# Performance Reference (Vue)

Use this reference when optimizing Vue and Nuxt applications.
For general performance principles (Core Web Vitals definitions, bundle budgets, runtime patterns), see `references/performance.md`.
This file covers Vue and Nuxt-specific optimizations.

---

## Core Web Vitals in Nuxt

### LCP — Largest Contentful Paint

```vue
<!-- Preload critical images with NuxtImg or useHead -->
<script setup lang="ts">
useHead({
  link: [
    { rel: "preload", as: "image", href: "/hero.webp" },
  ],
});
</script>

<!-- NuxtImage with priority -->
<template>
  <NuxtImg src="/hero.webp" alt="..." preload />
</template>
```

```vue
<!-- Server-render above-the-fold content (default in Nuxt) -->
<script setup lang="ts">
// This runs on the server — no loading state needed for initial render
const { data: hero } = await useFetch("/api/hero");
</script>

<template>
  <section>
    <h1>{{ hero?.title }}</h1>
    <p>{{ hero?.subtitle }}</p>
  </section>
</template>
```

### CLS — Cumulative Layout Shift

```vue
<!-- Always set dimensions on images -->
<NuxtImg src="/photo.jpg" alt="..." width="800" height="600" />

<!-- Reserve space for async content -->
<div :style="{ aspectRatio: '16/9' }">
  <NuxtImg src="/banner.jpg" alt="..." style="width: 100%; height: 100%; object-fit: cover;" />
</div>

<!-- Use placeholder while images load -->
<NuxtImg src="/photo.jpg" alt="..." placeholder loading="lazy" />
```

### INP — Interaction to Next Paint

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
import { ref, shallowRef, triggerRef } from "vue";

// Use shallowRef for large lists — avoids deep reactivity tracking
const items = shallowRef<Item[]>([]);

// Debounce expensive filtering
const query = ref("");
const debouncedQuery = refDebounced(query, 300); // from @vueuse/core

// Or use Web Worker for heavy computation
const { data: filtered } = useWebWorkerFn(
  (items, query) => items.filter((item) => matchesQuery(item, query)),
  { dependencies: [items, debouncedQuery] }
);
</script>
```

---

## Rendering Strategy in Nuxt

Choose per route with `routeRules`:

```ts
// nuxt.config.ts
export default defineNuxtConfig({
  routeRules: {
    // Static — generated at build time
    "/": { prerender: true },
    "/about": { prerender: true },
    "/blog/**": { prerender: true },

    // ISR — cached, revalidated on interval
    "/products/**": { swr: 3600 }, // Revalidate every hour

    // SSR — fresh on every request
    "/dashboard/**": { ssr: true },

    // SPA — client-only rendering
    "/admin/**": { ssr: false },

    // CDN cache
    "/api/public/**": {
      headers: { "cache-control": "s-maxage=3600" },
    },
  },
});
```

| Strategy | `routeRules` | When |
|---|---|---|
| **SSG** | `prerender: true` | Marketing, blog, docs — content doesn't change per-request |
| **ISR / SWR** | `swr: seconds` | Product pages, listings — periodic revalidation |
| **SSR** | `ssr: true` (default) | Personalized, real-time data |
| **SPA** | `ssr: false` | Admin panels, dashboards behind auth |
| **Hybrid** | Mix above per route | Most real apps |

---

## Image Optimization

### Nuxt Image

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
// nuxt.config.ts must have @nuxt/image module
</script>

<template>
  <!-- Automatic optimization, responsive sizes -->
  <NuxtImg
    src="/product.jpg"
    alt="Product photo"
    width="400"
    height="300"
    sizes="sm:100vw md:50vw lg:400px"
    format="webp"
    quality="80"
    loading="lazy"
  />

  <!-- Above the fold — preload -->
  <NuxtImg src="/hero.jpg" alt="..." preload />

  <!-- Background image with NuxtPicture for art direction -->
  <NuxtPicture
    src="/banner.jpg"
    alt="Banner"
    sizes="sm:100vw lg:1200px"
    :imgAttrs="{ style: 'width: 100%; height: 100%; object-fit: cover;' }"
  />
</template>
```

### Provider Configuration

```ts
// nuxt.config.ts
export default defineNuxtConfig({
  image: {
    // Local optimization
    quality: 80,
    format: ["webp", "avif"],

    // Or use a cloud provider
    provider: "cloudinary",
    cloudinary: {
      baseURL: "https://res.cloudinary.com/your-cloud/image/upload/",
    },
  },
});
```

---

## Font Loading

### Nuxt Fonts Module

```ts
// nuxt.config.ts
export default defineNuxtConfig({
  modules: ["@nuxt/fonts"],
  fonts: {
    families: [
      { name: "Inter", provider: "google", weights: [400, 500, 600, 700] },
    ],
    defaults: {
      fallbacks: ["system-ui", "-apple-system", "sans-serif"],
    },
  },
});
```

### Manual Self-Hosting

```css
/* assets/css/fonts.css */
@font-face {
  font-family: "Brand";
  src: url("/fonts/brand-regular.woff2") format("woff2");
  font-weight: 400;
  font-display: swap;
}

@font-face {
  font-family: "Brand";
  src: url("/fonts/brand-bold.woff2") format("woff2");
  font-weight: 700;
  font-display: swap;
}
```

```ts
// nuxt.config.ts
export default defineNuxtConfig({
  css: ["~/assets/css/fonts.css"],
  app: {
    head: {
      link: [
        {
          rel: "preload",
          as: "font",
          href: "/fonts/brand-regular.woff2",
          type: "font/woff2",
          crossorigin: "anonymous",
        },
      ],
    },
  },
});
```

---

## Bundle Optimization

### Analyze Bundle

```bash
# Nuxt bundle analysis
npx nuxi analyze
```

### Component-Level Code Splitting

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
import { defineAsyncComponent } from "vue";

// Lazy load heavy components
const HeavyChart = defineAsyncComponent({
  loader: () => import("./HeavyChart.vue"),
  loadingComponent: ChartSkeleton,
  delay: 200,
});
</script>

<template>
  <Suspense>
    <HeavyChart :data="chartData" />
    <template #fallback>
      <ChartSkeleton />
    </template>
  </Suspense>
</template>
```

### Client-Only Components

```vue
<!-- HeavyEditor.client.vue — only included in client bundle -->
<script setup lang="ts">
// Browser-only code is safe here
import { EditorView } from "@codemirror/view";
</script>

<!-- Or use ClientOnly wrapper -->
<template>
  <ClientOnly>
    <HeavyInteractiveMap :markers="markers" />
    <template #fallback>
      <MapSkeleton />
    </template>
  </ClientOnly>
</template>
```

### Tree-Shaking and Import Optimization

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
// Good — import only what you need
import { format } from "date-fns/format";
import { debounce } from "lodash-es/debounce";

// Bad — imports entire library
import { format } from "date-fns";
import _ from "lodash";

// Better — use native APIs
const formatted = new Intl.DateTimeFormat("en-US", { dateStyle: "medium" }).format(date);
</script>
```

### Nuxt Module Optimization

```ts
// nuxt.config.ts
export default defineNuxtConfig({
  // Only include modules you actually use
  modules: [
    "@nuxt/image",
    "@nuxt/fonts",
    "@pinia/nuxt",
  ],

  // Reduce Nitro bundle
  nitro: {
    minify: true,
    compressPublicAssets: true,
  },

  // Experimental features for smaller bundles
  experimental: {
    payloadExtraction: true,  // Extract payload for static pages
    treeshakeClientOnly: true, // Remove server-only code from client
  },
});
```

---

## Reactivity Performance

### Avoid Deep Reactivity for Large Data

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
import { shallowRef, triggerRef } from "vue";

// 10,000 items — shallowRef prevents Vue from tracking every nested property
const items = shallowRef<Item[]>([]);

// Update by replacing reference
function addItem(item: Item) {
  items.value = [...items.value, item];
}

// If you must mutate, trigger manually
function updateItem(index: number, updates: Partial<Item>) {
  Object.assign(items.value[index], updates);
  triggerRef(items);
}
</script>
```

### `v-memo` for Expensive Lists

```vue
<template>
  <div
    v-for="item in items"
    :key="item.id"
    v-memo="[item.id, item.selected, item.updatedAt]"
  >
    <!-- Only re-renders when memo deps change -->
    <ExpensiveItemCard :item="item" />
  </div>
</template>
```

### `v-once` for Static Content

```vue
<template>
  <!-- Rendered once, never patched -->
  <footer v-once>
    <p>&copy; 2026 Company. All rights reserved.</p>
    <nav aria-label="Legal">
      <a href="/privacy">Privacy</a>
      <a href="/terms">Terms</a>
    </nav>
  </footer>
</template>
```

### Computed vs Method

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
import { computed } from "vue";

// Computed — cached, recalculates only when dependencies change
const sortedItems = computed(() =>
  [...items.value].sort((a, b) => a.name.localeCompare(b.name))
);

// Method — runs every render, no caching
function getSortedItems() {
  return [...items.value].sort((a, b) => a.name.localeCompare(b.name));
}

// Use computed in templates, methods for event handlers
</script>

<template>
  <!-- Good — cached -->
  <ItemList :items="sortedItems" />

  <!-- Bad — recomputes every render cycle -->
  <ItemList :items="getSortedItems()" />
</template>
```

---

## Virtual Scrolling

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
import { useVirtualList } from "@vueuse/core";

const { list, containerProps, wrapperProps } = useVirtualList(items, {
  itemHeight: 64,
});
</script>

<template>
  <div v-bind="containerProps" style="height: 400px; overflow: auto;">
    <div v-bind="wrapperProps">
      <div v-for="{ data: item, index } in list" :key="item.id" style="height: 64px;">
        <ItemRow :item="item" />
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</template>
```

Alternative: use `@tanstack/vue-virtual` for more complex cases.

---

## Third-Party Scripts

```ts
// nuxt.config.ts — use @nuxtjs/scripts module
export default defineNuxtConfig({
  modules: ["@nuxt/scripts"],
  scripts: {
    globals: {
      // Analytics — load after hydration
      analytics: {
        src: "https://analytics.example.com/script.js",
        trigger: "onNuxtReady",
      },
    },
  },
});
```

```vue
<!-- Or use useScript composable -->
<script setup lang="ts">
const { status } = useScript("https://chat.example.com/widget.js", {
  trigger: "idle", // Load when browser is idle
});
</script>
```

```vue
<!-- Manual approach with useHead -->
<script setup lang="ts">
useHead({
  script: [
    {
      src: "https://analytics.example.com/script.js",
      defer: true,
      tagPosition: "bodyClose", // Load at end of body
    },
  ],
});
</script>
```

---

## Monitoring Web Vitals

```ts
// plugins/web-vitals.client.ts
import { onCLS, onINP, onLCP } from "web-vitals";

export default defineNuxtPlugin(() => {
  onCLS((metric) => reportVital(metric));
  onINP((metric) => reportVital(metric));
  onLCP((metric) => reportVital(metric));
});

function reportVital(metric: { name: string; value: number; rating: string }) {
  // Send to your analytics service
  if (navigator.sendBeacon) {
    navigator.sendBeacon("/api/vitals", JSON.stringify(metric));
  }
}
```

---

## Checklist

- [ ] LCP image uses `preload` or `<NuxtImg preload />`
- [ ] All images have explicit dimensions or aspect-ratio (no CLS)
- [ ] Fonts use `font-display: swap` and are self-hosted or use `@nuxt/fonts`
- [ ] `routeRules` are configured per route (SSG, ISR, SSR, SPA)
- [ ] Heavy components use `defineAsyncComponent` or `.client.vue`
- [ ] Large reactive data uses `shallowRef`, not deep `ref`
- [ ] Expensive list items use `v-memo`
- [ ] `computed` is used instead of methods in templates for derived data
- [ ] Long lists (100+ items) use virtual scrolling
- [ ] Third-party scripts load with `defer`, `idle`, or `onNuxtReady`
- [ ] Bundle is analyzed with `npx nuxi analyze`
- [ ] Web Vitals are monitored in production
